title: Kinetic Theory of Gases Test - 2 (Topic Test)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: An object is placed between infinity and the pole (P) of a convex mirror. The position of the image is
options: between pole (P) and the focus (F), behind the mirror
between the focus (F) and infinity, behind the mirror
between the pole (P) and the infinity, in front of the mirror
at the focus (F), behind the mirror
questions.solution:
For a convex mirror the mirror‐formula

$$\frac1u + \frac1v = \frac1f$$

applies, with

$u>0$ (object in front of mirror),

$f<0$ (convex mirror focal length),

$v<0$ (image behind mirror).

If $u\to\infty$, then $1/v=1/f$ ⇒ $v=f$ (image at the focus, behind).

As $u$ decreases from ∞ toward 0, $\;1/v=1/f-1/u$ becomes more negative ⇒ $|v|$ decreases from $|f|$ toward 0.

So for any finite object distance the image lies between the pole $P$ and the focus $F$, behind the mirror.

Answer: Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire at a point outside the wire depends
options: inversely on the distance from it
directly on the distance from it
inversely at short distances and directly at large distances from it
directly on the distance (at short distances) and inversely on the distance (at long distances) from it

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A simple pendulum having bob of mass m and length of string l has time period of T. If the mass of the bob is doubled and the length of the string is halved, then the time period of this pendulum will be
questions.solution:
The time period $ T $ of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$$ T = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{l}{g}} $$

where:

  • $ l $ is the length of the string.
  • $ g $ is the acceleration due to gravity.

Notice that the mass $ m $ of the bob does not appear in this formula, indicating that the time period of a simple pendulum is independent of the mass of the bob.

Now, let's consider the modified pendulum where the mass of the bob is doubled and the length of the string is halved.

The new length of the string $ l' $ is:

$$ l' = \frac{l}{2} $$

The new time period $ T' $ using the modified length is:

$$ T' = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{l'}{g}} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{\frac{l}{2}}{g}} = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{l}{2g}} = 2\pi \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sqrt{\frac{l}{g}} = \frac{T}{\sqrt{2}} $$

Thus, the new time period of the pendulum is:

Option B: $ \frac{T}{\sqrt{2}} $

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The work done in moving a charge of 2 coulomb (C) from point A to point B is 24 J. What is the potential difference between A and B?
questions.solution:
The potential difference between A and B is given by

$$ \Delta V \;=\;\frac{W}{q} $$

where

• $W = 24\text{ J}$ is the work done, and

• $q = 2\text{ C}$ is the charge.

Plugging in,

$$ \Delta V = \frac{24}{2} = 12\text{ V}. $$

So the correct choice is 12 V.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
questions.solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: We hear an echo due to
questions.solution:
Concept:

Echo: An echo occurs when a sound wave reflects off a surface and is heard a short time after the original sound. This delay is caused by the time it takes for the sound wave to travel to the surface and back.

Reflection of sound: When sound waves encounter a hard surface, they bounce back, producing a reflected sound wave.

Conditions for Echo Formation:

The distance between the sound source and the reflecting surface should be at least 17.2 meters, assuming the speed of sound is approximately 344 m/s.

The reflecting surface should be large and hard to effectively reflect the sound.

Therefore, the phenomenon of hearing an echo is explained by the reflection of sound waves.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one among the. following figures correctly represents the ray diagram ? (Consider the lens to be thin)
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Ray Diagrams for Lenses

A ray diagram is a drawing that shows how light rays move through a lens.

There are three main rays we usually draw for a thin lens:

First Ray: If a ray comes in parallel to the main (principal) axis, it will bend and pass through the lens's focus on the other side.

Second Ray: If a ray goes through the center of the lens, it travels straight and does not bend.

Third Ray: If a ray goes toward the focus before reaching the lens, it will bend after passing through the lens and move out parallel to the main axis.

EXPLANATION:

For a convex lens (one that bulges out):

- A ray that comes in parallel to the main axis will bend and go through the focus on the other side.

- A ray that travels through the middle of the lens goes straight and does not change direction.

- A ray that heads toward the focus before entering the lens will come out of the lens going parallel to the main axis.

So, the right figure for the ray diagram is Option 3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about speed and velocity is correct?
options: Speed and velocity both are vector quantities.
Speed and velocity both are scalar quantities.
Speed is vector quantity and velocity is scalar quantity.
Speed is scalar quantity and velocity is vector quantity.
questions.solution:
The correct statement about speed and velocity is:

Option D: Speed is a scalar quantity and velocity is a vector quantity.

Here's a detailed explanation:

Speed: Speed is a scalar quantity. This means it only has magnitude and no direction. Speed is the rate at which an object covers distance. For example, if a car travels 60 kilometers per hour, this value indicates how fast the car is moving but does not specify the direction.

Velocity: Velocity, on the other hand, is a vector quantity. This means it includes both magnitude and direction. Velocity is the rate at which an object changes its position. For example, a car moving 60 kilometers per hour to the north has a velocity that specifies both the speed and the direction of movement.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: In a Telescope, compared to the eyepiece lens the objective lens has
options: larger focal length and larger aperture
larger focal length and smaller aperture
smaller focal length and larger aperture
smaller focal length and smaller aperture
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 1: larger focal length and larger aperture}$$

Step-by-step:

  1. In a telescope, the objective lens is the lens facing the distant object.

  2. Its job is to collect as much light as possible from faraway objects.

  3. So, it must have a large aperture.

Also:

  1. The objective lens forms a real, inverted, and diminished image of the distant object.

  2. For better magnification in a telescope, the objective lens should have a large focal length.

  3. The eyepiece acts like a magnifying glass, so it has a small focal length.

Therefore, compared to the eyepiece, the objective lens has:

  • larger focal length

  • larger aperture

Hence, the answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1}}$$

Option 3: Sound Navigation and Ranging

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A 100 g sphere is moving at a speed of 20 m/s and collides with another. sphere of mass 50 g. If the second sphere was at rest prior to the collision and the first sphere comes at rest immediately after the collision, considering the collision to be elastic, the speed of the second sphere would be
questions.solution:
To determine the speed of the second sphere after an elastic collision, we can use the principle of conservation of momentum and the fact that kinetic energy is conserved in an elastic collision. Let's denote the masses and velocities of the spheres as follows:

Mass of the first sphere, $$m_1 = 100 \, \text{g} = 0.1 \, \text{kg}$$

Initial velocity of the first sphere, $$u_1 = 20 \, \text{m/s}$$

Mass of the second sphere, $$m_2 = 50 \, \text{g} = 0.05 \, \text{kg}$$

Initial velocity of the second sphere, $$u_2 = 0 \, \text{m/s}$$

Final velocity of the first sphere, $$v_1 = 0 \, \text{m/s}$$

We need to find the final velocity of the second sphere, $$v_2$$.

Using the conservation of momentum:

$$m_1 u_1 + m_2 u_2 = m_1 v_1 + m_2 v_2$$

Substituting the given values:

$$0.1 \times 20 + 0.05 \times 0 = 0.1 \times 0 + 0.05 \times v_2$$

$$2 = 0.05 \times v_2$$

Solving for $$v_2$$:

$$v_2 = \frac{2}{0.05} = 40 \, \text{m/s}$$

Therefore, the speed of the second sphere after the collision is 40 m/s. The correct answer is:

Option D

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard. It verifies that
options: sound travels much faster than light
light travels much faster than sound
light and sound both travel with same speed
intensity of flash of lightning is very high during thunderstorm
questions.solution:
The phenomenon where the flash of lightning is seen before the thunderstorm is heard can be explained by the difference in the speeds at which light and sound travel. Light travels significantly faster than sound. Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: light travels much faster than sound

To elaborate, light travels at an approximate speed of $$3 \times 10^8 \, \text{m/s}$$ in a vacuum, and slightly slower in the atmosphere. On the other hand, sound travels at a much slower speed of approximately $$343 \, \text{m/s}$$ in air at room temperature. Because of this large discrepancy in speeds, the light from the lightning is observed almost instantaneously, while the sound waves take a longer time to reach the observer's ears. This delay is why we see the flash of lightning before we hear the thunder.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: SONAR is a device that is used to measure the distance of underwater objects by a ship. Which of the following types of waves does it use for this purpose?
options: Sound waves in audible range for human beings
questions.solution:
SONAR, which stands for Sound Navigation and Ranging, is a device used to measure the distance of underwater objects. The correct type of waves that SONAR uses for this purpose is:

Option C: Ultrasonic waves

Ultrasonic waves are sound waves with frequencies higher than the audible range for human beings (greater than 20 kHz). These waves are particularly effective for underwater detection because they can travel long distances with high precision and can easily reflect off objects, providing accurate distance measurements.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: One block of 2⋅0 kg mass is placed on top of another block of 3⋅0 kg mass. The coefficient of static friction between the two blocks is 0⋅2. The bottom block is pulled with a horizontal force F such that both the blocks move together without slipping. Taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>, the maximum value of the frictional force is :
questions.solution:
To determine the maximum value of the frictional force that allows the two blocks to move together without slipping, we need to consider the forces acting between the two blocks.

The maximum static frictional force $$ (f_{s,\text{max}}) $$ can be calculated using the formula:

$$ f_{s,\text{max}} = \mu_s \cdot N $$

Where:

  • $$ \mu_s $$ is the coefficient of static friction, which is 0.2 in this case.
  • $$ N $$ is the normal force exerted by the top block on the bottom block.

The normal force $$ (N) $$ is equal to the weight of the top block. We can calculate this as follows:

$$ N = m \cdot g $$

Where:

  • $$ m $$ is the mass of the top block, which is 2.0 kg.
  • $$ g $$ is the acceleration due to gravity, which is 10 m/s2.

Substituting the values, we get:

$$ N = 2.0 \, \text{kg} \times 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 = 20 \, \text{N} $$

Now, we can find the maximum static frictional force:

$$ f_{s,\text{max}} = 0.2 \times 20 \, \text{N} = 4 \, \text{N} $$

Thus, the maximum value of the frictional force is:

Option C: 4 N
